$comment: Actio macro layer. This is the ONLY hand-authored schema surface. `scripts/build-schema.mjs` merges it onto the vendored official GitHub Actions workflow schema (vendor/github-workflow.json) to produce actio.schema.json. Edit macros here, then run `npm run schema:build -w actio-core`.

meta.title: Actio workflow (.actio.yml)
meta.description: Source language for the Actio transpiler: the official GitHub Actions workflow schema (inherited wholesale from SchemaStore) plus Actio macro keywords (fragments/inject, retry, dynamic_matrix, fallback, for_each) that compile into standard workflow YAML. Every standard GitHub Actions key, event, and value is validated and autocompleted from the upstream schema; the macro keys are additive. See https://github.com/austenstone/actio.

fragments.title: fragments (Actio macro)
fragments.description: Actio macro. Reusable, named lists of steps. Splice a fragment into a job with `- inject: <name>`. The `fragments` key is stripped from the generated workflow.
fragments.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: reusable, named step lists.

Define ordered step lists once, then splice them into any job with `- inject: <name>`. The `fragments` key is stripped from the generated workflow.

```yaml
fragments:
  setup:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
jobs:
  build:
    steps:
      - inject: setup
      - run: npm ci
```

additionalProperties.description: An ordered list of steps to inject wherever this fragment is referenced.

params.description: Actio macro. Typed compile-time parameters. References are resolved with `{{ params.<name> }}` or `{{ toJSON(params.<name>) }}` and stripped from emitted workflow YAML.

job_defaults.description: Actio macro. Default job-level settings merged into jobs at compile time. Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

executors.description: Actio macro. Named runner/container/service presets referenced by `jobs.<id>.executor`. Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

injectionHoist.description: Actio macro. Global default injection-hoist mode for every `run:` in the workflow. Overridden by job- and step-level `injectionHoist`. Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

finally.title: finally (Actio macro)
finally.description: Actio macro. Workflow-scoped teardown. Each named entry compiles to an aggregator job that `needs:` every real job and runs on the normal path (`if: !cancelled()`), plus an auto cancel-companion (`if: cancelled()`, `timeout-minutes: 5`). Nest `on_success`/`on_failure`/`on_abort` for outcome-branch jobs. Use `ensure:` for job/step-scoped teardown, not `finally:`.
finally.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: workflow-scoped teardown (sibling to `jobs:`).

Each named entry compiles to an aggregator job that `needs:` every real job and runs on the normal path (`if: !cancelled()`), plus an auto cancel-companion (`if: cancelled()`, `timeout-minutes: 5`). Nest `on_success`/`on_failure`/`on_abort` for outcome-branch jobs; `on_abort: []` opts out of the cancel job.

```yaml
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: ./deploy.sh
finally:
  teardown:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: ./cleanup.sh
```

For job/step teardown use `ensure:` instead.

inject.title: inject (Actio macro)
inject.description: Actio macro. Splice in the steps of the named fragment (defined under top-level `fragments:`) at this position. Replaced inline by `actio build`.
inject.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: splice a fragment here.

Replaced inline by `actio build` with the steps of the named fragment from top-level `fragments:`.

```yaml
steps:
  - inject: setup
  - run: npm test
```

static_if.title: static_if (Actio macro)
static_if.description: Actio macro. Compile-time structural condition. If false, this entire step map is omitted from emitted workflow YAML.
static_if.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: compile-time structural condition.

Evaluated at transpile time against compile-time roots (`params.*`, `for_each.*`, `define.*`). If the expression is false the entire step is removed from the generated workflow; if true the `static_if` key is stripped and the rest is emitted verbatim. Use `if:` for runtime conditions.

```yaml
steps:
  - name: Publish preview
    static_if: params.deploy
    run: ./publish.sh
```

unsafe.description: Actio macro (injection-hoist). Opt this step out of injection-hoist entirely (`${{ }}` left inline in `run:`). Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

trust.description: Actio macro (injection-hoist). Context paths that should NOT be hoisted out of `run:` even if untrusted (you vouch for them). Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

force.description: Actio macro (injection-hoist). Context paths to hoist into `env:` even if classified trusted. Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

ensure.title: ensure (Actio macro)
ensure.description: Actio macro. Same-step teardown. The listed steps are spliced in right after this step, each guarded with `if: always()`, so they run even if the step fails or is cancelled.

on_success.title: on_success (Actio macro)
on_success.description: Actio macro. Steps to run after this step only when the step succeeded. Guarded with `if: success() && steps.<id>.outcome == 'success'`.

on_failure.title: on_failure (Actio macro)
on_failure.description: Actio macro. Steps to run after this step only when the step failed. Guarded with `if: !cancelled() && steps.<id>.outcome == 'failure'` (keys on outcome, pre-continue-on-error).

on_abort.title: on_abort (Actio macro)
on_abort.description: Actio macro. Steps to run after this step on cancellation. At step scope this only sees step-level cancellation; run-level cancel belongs in a workflow `finally:`.

^static_if\\(.+\\)$.description: Actio macro. Conditional merge map. When expression is true, merge this mapping into the parent map.

static_if.title: static_if (Actio macro)
static_if.description: Actio macro. Compile-time structural condition. If false, this entire job map is omitted from emitted workflow YAML.
static_if.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: compile-time structural condition.

Evaluated at transpile time against compile-time roots (`params.*`, `for_each.*`, `define.*`). If the expression is false the entire job is removed from the generated workflow; if true the `static_if` key is stripped and the rest is emitted verbatim. Use `if:` for runtime conditions.

```yaml
jobs:
  release:
    static_if: params.deploy
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: ./release.sh
```

ensure.title: ensure (Actio macro)
ensure.description: Actio macro. Same-job teardown. The listed steps are appended to this job, each guarded with `if: always()`, so they run even if earlier steps fail or the job is cancelled (bounded by the job timeout).

on_success.title: on_success (Actio macro)
on_success.description: Actio macro. Steps appended to this job that run only when the job succeeded. Guarded with `if: success()`.

on_failure.title: on_failure (Actio macro)
on_failure.description: Actio macro. Steps appended to this job that run only when the job failed. Guarded with `if: failure()`.

on_abort.title: on_abort (Actio macro)
on_abort.description: Actio macro. Steps appended to this job that run only when the job was cancelled. Guarded with `if: cancelled()` (bounded by the job timeout).

executor.description: Actio macro. Name (or ordered list of names) from top-level `executors:`. Expanded during compile and stripped from output.

^static_if\\(.+\\)$.description: Actio macro. Conditional merge map. When expression is true, merge this mapping into the parent map.
Actio macro. Conditional merge map. When expression is true, merge this mapping into the parent map.

ifChanged.title: if_changed (Actio macro)
ifChanged.description: Actio macro. Gate this step or job on file changes. Runs only when files changed in the pull request or push match one of these glob patterns. Compiles to a single shared `dorny/paths-filter` setup job plus a `needs.<setup>.outputs.<flag> == 'true'` guard folded into the existing `if:`. Stripped from generated workflow YAML.
ifChanged.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: file-change gating.

Runs this step or job only when files changed in the pull request or push match one of the glob patterns. Actio synthesizes one shared setup job that runs [`dorny/paths-filter`](https://github.com/dorny/paths-filter) once, publishes a flag output per unique glob group, and folds `if: needs.<setup>.outputs.<flag> == 'true'` (AND-combined with any existing `if:`) plus `needs:` into each guarded node.

```yaml
steps:
  - name: Build docs
    run: ./build-docs.sh
    if_changed:
      - 'docs/**'
      - '*.md'
```

share.title: share (Actio macro)
share.description: Actio macro. Publish step outputs so other jobs can consume them. Each entry maps an output `<name>` to a scalar value (value-form) or a captured command (`run`, capture-form). Reference anywhere with `${{ share.<name> }}`; Actio wires `job.outputs` and `needs` automatically and strips the `share` key from the generated workflow.
share.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: publish step outputs cross-job.

Each entry maps an output `<name>` to a value. Reference it anywhere with `${{ share.<name> }}` and Actio auto-wires `job.outputs` + `needs`.

- **value-form** — scalar shorthand or `{ value, required?, type? }`.
- **capture-form** — `{ run, json?, type? }` captures command stdout via a heredoc.
- **json: true** lets consumers read fields: `${{ share.<name>.<field> }}` lowers to `fromJSON(...)`.

```yaml
steps:
  - run: VERSION=$(jq -r .version package.json)
    share:
      version: $VERSION
```

sharedOutput.title: share.<name> (Actio macro)
sharedOutput.description: A shared output definition: a scalar shorthand, a value-form object (`value`), or a capture-form object (`run`).

oneOf.description: Scalar shorthand value-form: the shell value to publish (e.g. `$VERSION`).

value.description: The shell value to publish as this output.

required.description: Opt-in runtime guard. When true, Actio appends a non-empty assertion that fails the step with `::error::empty share value` if the value is blank.

json.description: Declare the value as JSON so consumers can read fields via `${{ share.<name>.<field> }}` (lowered to `fromJSON(...)`).

run.description: Capture-form: command whose stdout is captured into this output via a heredoc (`ACTIO_EOF_<name>`).

json.description: Declare the captured value as JSON so consumers can read fields via `${{ share.<name>.<field> }}` (lowered to `fromJSON(...)`).

required.description: Opt-in runtime guard for empty captured output.

sharedOutputType.description: Compile-time type assertion for the shared output. Boolean is a type assertion only — Actio does NOT coerce the runtime value.

default.description: JSON-like default value. Use `{{ toJSON(params.<name>) }}` for string interpolation.

retry.title: retry (Actio macro)
retry.description: Actio macro. Retry a flaky step with optional backoff; expands into a chain of conditional attempts where attempt N runs only if attempt N-1 failed. Shorthand `retry: 3` is the same as `retry: { attempts: 3 }`.
retry.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: retry a flaky step.

Expands into a chain of conditional attempts: attempt _N_ runs only if attempt _N-1_ failed. Shorthand `retry: 3` is the same as `retry: { attempts: 3 }`.

```yaml
steps:
  - run: flaky-command
    retry:
      attempts: 3
      delay: 10s
```

delay.description: Backoff between attempts: a duration like "10s", "2m", "1h", or a number of seconds. Injects `sleep` steps between attempts.

fallback.title: fallback (Actio macro)
fallback.description: Actio macro. A native try/catch. Default (notify): the fallback runs on failure via `if: failure()` but the job still fails. With `recover: true` the guarded step gets `continue-on-error: true` and the fallback keys off `outcome`, so the job can pass.
fallback.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: a native try/catch.

- **notify** (default): the fallback runs on failure via `if: failure()`, but the job still fails.
- **recover** (`recover: true`): the guarded step gets `continue-on-error: true` and the fallback keys off `outcome`, so the job can pass.

```yaml
steps:
  - run: deploy
    fallback:
      recover: true
      steps:
        - run: ./rollback.sh
```

oneOf.description: Fallback steps (notify mode). The job still fails.

steps.description: Steps to run when the guarded step or job fails.

recover.description: When true, swallow the guarded failure (continue-on-error) so the job can continue. Step-level only; ignored on job-level fallback.

dynamicMatrix.title: dynamic_matrix (Actio macro)
dynamicMatrix.description: Actio macro. Build a matrix at runtime: `script` prints a JSON array (or `{include:[...]}`) to stdout. Actio generates a setup job that publishes the JSON and wires this job to consume it via `fromJSON()` with a fail-fast:false default and an empty-matrix guard.
dynamicMatrix.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: build a matrix at runtime.

`script` prints a JSON array (or `{ include: [...] }`) to stdout. Actio generates a setup job that publishes the JSON and wires this job to consume it via `fromJSON()`, with `fail-fast: false` and an empty-matrix guard by default.

```yaml
jobs:
  test:
    dynamic_matrix:
      script: ./list-targets.sh
      alias: target
    steps:
      - run: ./test ${{ matrix.target }}
```

script.description: Command or local script path that prints a JSON array (or `{"include":[...]}`) to stdout.

alias.description: Wrap a scalar JSON array under `matrix.<alias>` (referenced as `${{ matrix.<alias> }}`). Omit for raw `{include:[...]}` mode.

runs-on.description: Runner for the generated setup job. Defaults to the job's `runs-on`, else `ubuntu-latest`.

shell.description: Shell that runs `script` in the setup job, like a step's `shell`. Supported: bash, sh, pwsh, powershell, python. Actio emits matching plumbing to publish the matrix output; other shells (e.g. cmd) are rejected.

compact.description: Pipe the script's stdout through `jq -c .` to normalize the matrix JSON to one line. POSIX-only (bash/sh); ignored for pwsh/powershell/python, which emit raw multi-line output that `fromJSON` parses fine. Defaults to true.

checkout.description: Check out the repository in the setup job. Defaults to true when `script` is a local path.

fail-fast.description: Value for `strategy.fail-fast` on the matrix job. Defaults to false.

id.description: Override the generated setup job id.

injectionHoistMode.title: injectionHoist mode (Actio macro)
injectionHoistMode.description: Actio macro. Injection-hoist behavior for `run:` `${{ }}` interpolation: `fix` (hoist untrusted values into env and rewrite the body, default), `warn`, `error`, or `off`. Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

lifecycleHook.title: lifecycle hook (Actio macro)
lifecycleHook.description: Actio macro. An ordered list of teardown/outcome steps attached to a step or job. Compiled into guarded steps and stripped from the generated workflow.

on_success.description: Actio macro. Named teardown jobs that run only when the workflow succeeded (`if: success()`).

on_failure.description: Actio macro. Named teardown jobs that run only when the workflow failed (`if: failure()`).

on_abort.description: Actio macro. Cancel-path teardown. A mapping of named jobs replaces the auto cancel-companion; an empty list (`[]`) opts out of any cancel job while the normal-path aggregator still runs.

oneOf.description: Explicit opt-out: emit no cancel-path job.

finallyBranchGroup.description: Actio macro. A mapping of named teardown jobs for one outcome branch.

finallyJob.description: Actio macro. A teardown job. Accepts standard job keys plus the Actio `when:` sugar; Actio injects `needs:` over all real jobs and the appropriate outcome guard at compile time.

when.title: when (Actio macro)
when.description: Actio sugar inside a finally job. Gate on another job's result, e.g. `deploy.failed` → `needs.deploy.result == 'failure'`. States: failed, succeeded, cancelled, skipped. The referenced job is force-added to this job's `needs:`.
